items.title: Garcia glundert na 5 goals: "Als Lukaku er niet bij is, scoren anderen"
items.summary: Voor de wedstrijd tegen USA werden er heel wat vragen gesteld over hoe de Rode Duivels het moesten aanpakken zonder Romelu Lukaku. België scoorde liefst 5 keer zonder zijn topschutter en dat stemt coach Rudi Garcia bijzonder tevreden, hoorden we hem tijdens zijn persbabbel vertellen.

items.title: USMNT handed reality check by Doku, Belgium ahead of World Cup
items.summary: Belgium provided a sobering assessment of where the USMNT currently stands, doling out a heavy 5-2 defeat that will leave Mauricio Pochettino with plenty to ponder.

items.title: Player ratings: Weah poor as USMNT suffer heavy Belgium defeat
items.summary: It was a difficult game for the U.S. against Belgium, with Tim Weah struggling to make a positive impact.

items.title: USMNT routed by Belgium in blow to WCup prep
items.summary: The USMNT suffered a setback in its preparations for the World Cup as Belgium ran rampant in the second half to emerge with a 5-2 win at Atlanta's Mercedes-Benz Stadium on Saturday.

items.title: Pulisic, U.S. 'shocked' by Belgium jersey clash
items.summary: Christian Pulisic was among several U.S. players who said that the similarly colored jerseys worn by Belgium and the USMNT made it more difficult to pick out teammates.

items.title: Denver sets NWSL attendance record at Mile High
items.summary: Denver Summit set the NWSL attendance record in their inaugural home game, bringing in a crowd of 63,004 against the Washington Spirit.

items.title: Highlights: Leigh Leopards 21-20 Toulouse Olympique
items.summary: Highlights of the Super League clash between Leigh Leopards and Toulouse Olympique.

items.title: Woodland in pole position ahead of Hojgaard heading into Houston Open final round
items.summary: Gary Woodland will take a one‑shot lead over Nicolai Hojgaard into the final round of the Texas Children's Houston Open after a composed five‑under 65 moved him to 18 under.

items.title: VI Catwalk WK 2026: veel landen maken nieuwe shirts bekend
items.summary: Het WK in Canada, Mexico en de Verenigde Staten nadert met rasse schreden. Het vooruitblikken kan beginnen en dat doet VI op verschillende manieren. Zo zetten we in dit artikel de WK-tenues van landen op een rijtje.

items.title: Belgen niet in de polonaise na riante zege: 'Hebben ons goed herpakt'
items.summary: Bondscoach Rudi Garcia van België kijkt met een tevreden gevoel terug op de oefenzege op de Verenigde Staten . Na een moeizame start van het duel in Atlanta zegevierden de Rode Duivels uiteindelijk met ruime cijfers.

items.title: België zet met uitblinker Lammens en debutant Godts de VS voor schut
items.summary: België heeft zaterdagavond een riante oefenzege geboekt op de Verenigde Staten. De Amerikanen waren voor de pauze nog de betere ploeg, maar stuitten toen vaak op uitblinker Senne Lammens. Na de rust liepen de Rode Duivels zeer eenvoudig weg bij het mede-gastland van het komende WK. Ajacied Mika Godts maakte als invaller zijn debuut bij België.

items.title: Tuchel ziet acht spelers vertrekken uit trainingskamp Engeland
items.summary: Bondscoach Thomas Tuchel van Engeland heeft in aanloop naar het oefenduel met Japan zijn selectie noodgedwongen teruggebracht van 35 naar 27 spelers. Onder anderen Bukayo Saka en Declan Rice hebben na het gelijkspel tegen Uruguay het trainingskamp verlaten.

items.title: Fans booing Scotland surprise and disappoint Clarke
items.summary: Scotland head coach Steve Clarke takes positives from his side's friendly defeat by Japan at Hampden, but expresses disappointment at the boos that accompanied the full-time whistle.

items.title: Willem II is derde plek in KKD kwijt aan De Graafschap, Eindhoven verslaat Emmen
items.summary: Willem II heeft de pas heroverde derde plek in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie alweer moeten afstaan aan De Graafschap. Zaterdagavond ging de Tricoleres in eigen huis, ondanks een overvloed aan kansen, onderuit tegen de ploeg uit Doetinchem. FC Eindhoven versloeg FC Emmen met minimale cijfers en mag daardoor blijven dromen van een play-off-ticket.

items.title: 'Scotland's Hampden warriors return to reserved selves'
items.summary: Scotland were too flat and timid in defeat by Japan as the clock ticks towards their World Cup return, writes Tom English.

items.title: Sabalenka beats Gauff to retain Miami Open title and complete 'Sunshine Double'
items.summary: Defending champion Aryna Sabalenka beat hometown favourite Coco Gauff in ‌the Miami Open final to join an exclusive club by completing the coveted 'Sunshine Double'.

items.title: Russo 'stepping up' as Arsenal lead hunt for European places
items.summary: With Arsenal chasing a place in next season's Champions League, England striker Alessia Russo has hit form at just the right time - as her 22-minute Women's Super League hat-trick against Tottenham proves.

items.title: Tuchel names four centre-backs ahead of Maguire in pecking order
items.summary: England boss Thomas Tuchel has appeared to deliver a big blow to Harry Maguire's World Cup chances by insisting he is still at least the fifth-choice centre-back.

items.title: Alessia Russo’s rapid fire hat-trick sinks Tottenham and sends Arsenal second in WSL
items.summary: Arsenal secured their 11th consecutive victory in all competitions with a confident win against Tottenham at the Emirates Stadium on Saturday evening. Alessia Russo scored a 22-minute hat-trick to put her side in firm control while Caitlin Foord and Stina Blackstenius added more in the second half. It was a devastating first-half performance from Russo. Her three in the latest edition of the north London derby took her tally to five in the last eight days, a week in which Arsenal extended their unbeaten run in all competitions. Continue reading...

items.title: Leigh snatch golden-point glory after O'Brien drop goal
items.summary: Gareth O'Brien scored a golden-point drop goal to earn Leigh a dramatic 21-20 victory over Toulouse.

items.title: Were England players really 'playing for themselves' - and what next?
items.summary: Did England manager Thomas Tuchel judge this international break correctly by naming such a large squad? Or was it a missed opportunity?

items.title: Running on empty? Premier League teams falter under weight of endless schedule | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Players are not covering the distances of old – they are not being lazy but adapting to demands of an arduous campaign There is nothing English football admires more than honest endeavour, which is perhaps a consequence of the league’s origins in the industrial cities of the north and Midlands. “He put in a shift.” “She did her job.” “He gave his all.” The language of football is the language of the pit or the factory floor. All top-level players these days are supremely skilled, but still we demand that they be exhausted by the final whistle, legs leaden with effort, hair soaked with sweat. Which was why it seemed to cause such consternation when Alan Shearer mentioned on Match of the Day last Saturday that Chelsea have run less than their opponents in every Premier League game they have played this season. Continue reading...

items.title: Passive Scotland’s World Cup preparations hit by late defeat against Japan
items.summary: Scotland’s followers have discovered there is a drawback to World Cup qualification after all. Nobody who paid – and handsomely – to sit through the sheer tedium of this fixture could reasonably deny that. Yes, Scotland, you can come to the party; but only after a clutch of international friendlies. It is just about worth the trade-off. Officially, this marked the start of the tournament buildup for the Scots and Japan. It was a game that involved the going through of motions rather than one from which to draw strong conclusions. Japan were the slicker team, as should be expected for a nation which has aspirations of making a proper impact in the summer. Junya Ito ensured the visitors headed towards Wembley in fine fettle, his goal fitting reward for Japan’s enterprising approach. Continue reading...

items.title: Stones the exception to Tuchel’s World Cup rule despite cold shoulder from Guardiola
items.summary: England’s head coach still rates injury-prone Manchester City defender and seems likely to be a fundamental part of his squad this summer – if fit Every manager reserves the right to make an exception to the rules. For Thomas Tuchel, it is John Stones. The England head coach has watched Stones endure a lost season at Manchester City; another one, really, because things were similar for him last time out – certainly in terms of appearances. Once again, there have been injury problems, the sense that Stones cannot get himself fully right compounded over this past week with England. The 31-year-old struggled in training and when he felt something in a calf muscle on Thursday, Tuchel was forced to leave him out of the Wembley friendly against Uruguay on Friday night. He started Fikayo Tomori alongside Harry Maguire in central defence in a drab game that ended 1-1, while Stones has gone back to his club and will play no part against Japan on Tuesday. Continue reading...

items.title: Man City 'handling pressure' of WSL title race with 'statement' win
items.summary: Manchester City's manager Andree Jeglertz says he "enjoyed" his side's performance in a dominating win at Manchester United that edges them closer to a first WSL title in a decade.

items.title: Miedema propels Manchester City closer to WSL title with derby double against United
items.summary: The latest leg of Manchester City’s victory lap took them to Old Trafford and they did not simply show Manchester United why they are going to become the Women’s Super League champions. They tore them apart in the process, while also damaging United’s hopes of qualifying for Europe next season. The away side’s symbolic 3-0 win at the home of their embarrassed neighbours demonstrated their vast superiority, and perhaps the only surprising thing was that they did not score more than Vivianne Miedema’s two headers and Kerstin Casparij’s far-post finish from a Lauren Hemp cross. It could easily have been five or six, with Hemp hitting the bar before a disallowed Rebecca Knaak goal, which left everybody in the stadium confused, in a first half when the visitors carved open the home defence with ease. Continue reading...

items.title: Dodi Lukebakio openhartig na twee goals voor België: "De tranen stonden in mijn ogen"
items.summary: De Rode Duivels wonnen overtuigend met 2-5 van de Verenigde Staten. Na een moeizame start viel alles op zijn plaats, met Lukebakio als uitblinker en tweevoudig doelpuntenmaker

items.title: Des clubs de Jupiler Pro League en course pour recruter un jeune talent belge du PSV
items.summary: On retrouve traditionnellement pas mal de jeunes talents belges du côté du PSV. Nicolas Verkooijen en fait partie. Le verra-t-on bientôt à l'œuvre dans notre pays ?

items.title: 🎥 But de l'année de Goudani (Virton), match animé pour Mons, Namur gagne enfin : folle soirée en D1 ACFF !
items.summary: Cinquième journée des Playoffs de promotion en D1 ACFF, avec les rencontres de Mons et de Virton face à Meux et Rochefort. Dans les Playdowns, Namur était également en action ce soir.

items.title: 'JPL-clubs willen jonge Belg van PSV binnenhalen'
items.summary: Bij PSV lopen er traditioneel heel wat goede, jonge Belgen rond. Nicolas Verkooijen is er daar een van. Zien we hem binnenkort aan het werk in ons land?
